feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day, plus Polish public-holiday reminders

entry.title: The Uplift: The American Dream
entry.content: We celebrate the United States ahead of its 250th anniversary with stories about American heroes and pastimes. The founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics shares how he made a massive life change to become a Catholic priest.

entry.title: Trump imposes double-digit tariffs on dozens of countries as his 10% levies are set to expire Friday
entry.content: President Donald Trump is going ahead with new double-digit tariffs on dozens of U.S. trading partners just as the clock runs out Friday on stopgap levies he imposed after a stinging defeat at the Supreme Court

entry.title: Alaska sends 3,000 letters asking voters to verify their citizenship ahead of this year's elections
entry.content: Alaska has sent more than 3,000 letters to residents asking them to verify their citizenship ahead of this year's elections

entry.title: Federal judges allow new Tennessee congressional map that splits majority-Black district in Memphis
entry.content: A federal judicial panel has allowed a new Tennessee congressional map that splits apart a majority-Black district in Memphis

entry.title: Giant metal roosters spread autism acceptance in Maine after town zoning spat
entry.content: Giant metal roosters are popping up on lawns in and around a coastal Maine community and as far away as New Zealand in support of a 6-year-old autistic girl and her love of the colorful creations

entry.title: Meet the judges who will crown Australia’s next breakout startup
entry.content: TechCrunch Startup Battlefield is coming to Australia — and we're partnering with Stripe to find the country's most exciting early-stage startups.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Happiness likes to enter a house where there is good humor.
Lucky Numbers: 22, 39, 12, 88, 44, 62

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Prevent your bathroom mirror from fogging up after a hot shower with car wax.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Humans and dolphins are the only species that have sex for pleasure.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Robin Sharma
entry.content: “Change is hard at first, messy in the middle and gorgeous at the end.” — Robin Sharma

entry.title: Browns' Deshaun Watson, Shedeur Sanders fuel ongoing QB debate
entry.content: The big question is if Browns' Deshaun Watson or Shedeur Sanders get the start.

entry.title: Mobileye CEO Amnon Shashua to step aside as company pushes into robotaxis, robotics
entry.content: Shashua has been invited to take the chairman of the board seat.

entry.title: Scientists develop handheld device for measuring when your body is burning fat
entry.content: The breathalyzer identifies the amount of acetone in your breath to determine if your body is in ketosis.

entry.title: Is Anthony Edwards married? Timberwolves star takes big step with longtime girlfriend
entry.content: Here's the rundown on the Minnesota star's recently changed relationship status

entry.title: Black members of Oklahoma Native American tribe file federal lawsuit to gain citizenship rights
entry.content: Two descendants of Black people once enslaved by a Native American tribe are asking a federal court in Oklahoma to force tribal leaders to issue them citizenship cards as ordered a year ago by a tribal court

entry.title: Protesters burst in and take over news conference as Madison police identify man killed by officer a day earlier
entry.content: Protesters burst in and take over news conference as Madison police identify man killed by officer a day earlier

entry.title: Liberal Justice Elena Kagan says the Supreme Court is not a rubber stamp for Trump
entry.content: Justice Elena Kagan says the Supreme Court may be deeply conservative but it is not simply a rubber stamp for President Donald Trump

entry.title: D4vd's lawyers ask why more DNA testing wasn't done on chain saws, car in teen girl's killing
entry.content: Lawyers for D4vd began showing glimpses into their defense strategy Thursday, pressing police on why they didn't conduct more DNA testing on items found in the singer's home and in his car that authorities said were used in the killing of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez.

entry.title: 2026 World Matchplay of Darts Day 7 quarterfinals Live results, scores, schedule & bracket
entry.content: Only two more spots are up for grabs in the semifinals!

entry.title: Giant metal roosters spread autism acceptance in Maine after town zoning spat
entry.content: Giant metal roosters are popping up on lawns in and around a community in southern Maine and as far away as New Zealand in support of a 6-year-old autistic girl and her love of the colorful creations.

entry.title: What's next for Diego Pavia? Landing spots for former Heisman finalist, from second NFL chances to CFL to WWE
entry.content: Pavia once again faces an uncertain future after a brief stint in Baltimore.

entry.title: 2026 World Matchplay of Darts Day 6 quarterfinals final results, scores, schedule & bracket: Littler beats Rock, to play van Duijvenbode in semifinals
entry.content: The quarterfinals have arrived in Blackpool!

entry.title: Bears face lingering questions after Maxx Crobsy interest
entry.content: Maxx Crosby and the Bears reportedly talked during the offseason.

entry.title: When does Luke Littler play at the 2026 World Matchplay of Darts? Schedule, opponents and more
entry.content: Littler is looking to go back-to-back in Blackpool.

entry.title: 2026 World Matchplay of Darts: Draw, schedule, prize money and format explained
entry.content: One of the biggest majors of the year is here!

entry.title: Reality Bites Elon Musk and His Tesla, SpaceX Believers
entry.content: Shares of Tesla have tumbled around 15% on a disappointing quarter, and SpaceX has fallen 50% from its highs.

entry.title: 2 dead in flash floods caused by heavy rain in West Virginia, governor's office says
entry.content: Two people have died in flash floods caused by torrential rains in West Virginia this week, the governor's office said Thursday.

entry.title: Peacock is finally profitable, thanks to the World Cup and Love Island
entry.content: Gooooooooooool!

entry.title: First Nancy Guthrie ransom note read aloud on true-crime podcast
entry.content: The sender of the first ransom note in Nancy Guthrie's disappearance opened with a direct warning to her daughter, "Today" show co-anchor Savannah Guthrie, telling her that her mother's life "is in your hands."

entry.title: Tampa, Charleston airports to join new TSA privatized security initiative
entry.content: The new Gold+ initiative would allow private contractors to handle passenger screening at three airports while the TSA retains federal oversight.

entry.title: Black members of Oklahoma Native American tribe file federal lawsuit to gain citizenship rights
entry.content: Two descendants of Black people once enslaved by a Native American tribe are asking a federal court in Oklahoma to force tribal leaders to issue them citizenship cards as ordered a year ago by a tribal court.

entry.title: Peacock streaming service finally turns a profit as Comcast moves to spin off NBCUniversal
entry.content: NBCUniversal's Peacock turns a profit for the first time, boosted by audiences tuning into the World Cup and "Love Island."

entry.title: Measles outbreak "slowing," but newborn's family still can't meet her
entry.content: Utah's weekly measles case count has fallen. The national tally hasn't, and a newborn who's too young to be vaccinated is caught in between.

entry.title: DOJ to withdraw subpoenas for New York Times reporters over Air Force One story
entry.content: The New York Times moved to block subpoenas issued by the Justice Department for grand jury testimony and phone records from its reporters.

entry.title: Melinda French Gates Shares 3 Things Women Can Do to Better Advocate for Their Health
entry.content: On the first episode of "Healthful," Melinda French Gates shared three simple things women can do to better advocate for their health.

entry.title: Global oil prices hit $100 a barrel amid attacks in Red Sea
entry.content: The last time global oil prices settled above $100 was on May 22, data from FactSet shows.

entry.title: Trump talks plan to prevent ratepayers from shouldering costs of new AI data centers
entry.content: President Trump spoke Thursday about a plan to prevent ratepayers from taking on the costs of a new AI data centers. CBS News' Kelly O'Grady has more.

entry.title: Kevin Lyman on making Warped Tour the entry point for a new generation of festivalgoers
entry.content: Warped Tour founder Kevin Lyman talks about the second year of the festival's return to Long Beach with bigger stages but familiar controlled chaos — merch tents, skate ramps, surprises.

entry.title: Justice Department Agrees to Withdraw Subpoenas for New York Times Journalists
entry.content: A federal judge questioned whether prosecutors followed proper procedures in a criminal leak investigation.

entry.title: Consecutive Life Sentences in Minnesota Lawmaker Killings
entry.content: Vance Luther Boelter pleaded guilty to shooting two state Democratic lawmakers, killing Rep. Melissa Hortman and her husband.

entry.title: Hot faces fill the erotic thriller 'Her Private Hell,' but the plot is never in focus
entry.content: 'Drive' director Nicolas Winding Refn's first feature in a decade finds him still fixated on sizzling neon and chilly characters. He's got his kinks.

entry.title: Despite tax credits, filming in L.A. is still sluggish
entry.content: The number of shoot days from incentivized productions is slowly growing, but Los Angeles' on-location filming is still down compared to last year.

entry.title: How did 'Drive find that higher gear? The director says it was thanks to 'Sixteen Candles'
entry.content: For its 15th anniversary, 'Drive' director Nicolas Winding Refn looks back on working with Ryan Gosling, shooting in L.A. and some surprising influences.

entry.title: Fan-fave 'Love Island USA' sweethearts Olandria, Nic Vansteenberghe call it quits
entry.content: The summer sun has set on 'Love Island USA' fan favorites Olandria and Nic Vansteenberghe's romance, a year after their time on the hit series.

entry.title: Member of Vance’s Secret Service detail removed amid probe into alleged compromise of security info
entry.content: A member of JD Vance’s protective detail has been removed pending an investigation into allegations of compromising the vice president’s security, a Secret Service spokesman said Thursday.

entry.title: White House says Saudi Arabia nuclear deal contingent on joining Abraham Accords
entry.content: During a press briefing, White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt echoed a social media post from President Trump that said any nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia would be contingent on them joining the Abraham Accords. NBC News' Monica Alba reports from the White House on the potential deal and the White House's approach.

entry.title: Wisconsin governor calls for transparency after Madison officer fatally shoots man with knife
entry.content: The police shooting death of a man with a knife has roiled a Wisconsin city and prompted the governor to call for transparency and accountability in any investigation

entry.title: Your Guide to (Still) Eating Fresh Fruits and Vegetables This Summer
entry.content: An outbreak of cyclosporiasis that has sickened thousands shouldn’t prevent you from eating any fresh produce, experts say.

entry.title: FDA Advisory Committee to Review Peptide Restrictions Underway
entry.content: Peptides are often being promoted by wellness influencers and podcasters as treatments for everything from injuries and aging and now an FDA panel is meeting to discuss whether to loosen restrictions and make them easier to get. But critics say there's not enough data on safety and efficacy. NBC’s medical contributor Natalie Azar joins TODAY to break down the seven peptides being discussed and how effective they are.

entry.title: Get into med school in the graphic memoir 'See One, Do One, Teach One'
entry.content: Grace Farris' book brings readers behind the med school scenes — documenting anxiety over loans, bouts of impostor syndrome, friendships forged in stress, and the ever-impossible work-life balance.

entry.title: Trump says Saudi Arabia must normalize Israel ties before nuclear deal is approved
entry.content: President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal between the United States and Saudi Arabia requires the kingdom to normalize relations with Israel — a significant condition not previously mentioned as the deal was announced.

entry.title: Trump says Saudi nuclear pact requires kingdom to normalize Israel relations through Abraham Accords
entry.content: President Donald Trump says the Saudi nuclear agreement requires the kingdom to normalize relations with Israel through the Abraham Accords

entry.title: Protests Erupt After Fatal Police Shooting of Man in Wisconsin
entry.content: An investigation is underway after a police officer in Madison, Wisconsin, fatally shot a man who investigators say pulled a knife during a scuffle with deputies. Police say it took place after the man fled from officers who were responding to a report of an individual allegedly stealing bicycles and checking car doors. NBC’s Shaquille Brewster reports for TODAY. Warning: Video of the incident is disturbing.

entry.title: Man who pleaded guilty to killing top Minnesota Democratic lawmaker and her husband to be sentenced
entry.content: The Minnesota man who pleaded guilty to killing a top Democratic state lawmaker and her husband is due to be sentenced in federal court

entry.title: OpenAI says rogue AI models broke free from human control. Some see it as a 'warning shot'
entry.content: It is the kind of development once seen only in science fiction: An artificial intelligence system, trained to probe for digital vulnerabilities, breaks free of human control and acts on its own to hack another company

entry.title: This AI tool promises a 'second pair of eyes' to clinicians. Did patients benefit?
entry.content: Medical workers in a Nairobi clinic used an AI tool to check their work. A new study evaluates the result.

entry.title: This AI tool promises a 'second sight of eyes' to clinicians. Did patients benefit?
entry.content: Medical workers in a Nairobi clinic used an AI tool to check their work. A new study evaluates the result.

entry.title: New Concerns Over Oil Prices After Houthis Attack Saudi Tankers
entry.content: There are new concerns about the future of oil and gas prices after Yemen-based Houthi rebels, backed by Iran, attacked two Saudi Arabian oil tankers in the Red Sea. NBC’s Richard Engel reports on the new front in the Middle East conflict and Christine Romans joins TODAY with analysis on the economic impact to gas prices in the United States.

entry.title: Why health officials do not have a good handle on the current Ebola outbreak
entry.content: To get slightly ahead of the virus, health officials need to quickly identify people at risk of infection, a practice known as contact tracing. That's a challenge in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

entry.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Pushes West After Battering Louisiana
entry.content: Tropical Storm Bertha is pushing toward Texas after crashing ashore in Louisiana. The Gulf Coast was hammered with heavy rain, high winds and floods. Meanwhile, some areas are set to face triple digit temperatures through the weekend. NBC’s George Solis reports and TODAY’s Al Roker tracks the latest forecast.

entry.title: Blackstone’s Profit Surges on AI Investments
entry.content: Stronger inflows into the investment firm’s private-equity business offset a slowdown in private credit, where individual investors have pulled back.

entry.title: Number of cyclosporiasis cases in the U.S. has surpassed previous records, CDC says
entry.content: It can take weeks to confirm a single case of intestinal illness caused by the cyclospora parasite, but the CDC's tally for this year now stands at over 4,100.

entry.title: A Sign ‘Quality’ Investing Is Due for a Comeback
entry.content: Plus, is oil headed back above $100?

entry.title: Wall Street Firms Already Trade Trump’s Truth Social Feed. Now They Can Pay to Be Faster.
entry.content: Plus, the U.S. gives the president more muscular military options as he considers expanding the Iran war, and we go inside Taco Bell’s rush to contain cyclospora.

entry.title: Sony's FX5 cinema camera finally offers open gate and RAW 5K recording
entry.content: Sony has just unveiled its latest cinema camera that may foreshadow some long-awaited features in its consumer mirrorless lineup.

entry.title: Google adds selfie video as a log-in option
entry.content: You'll now be able to get into your Google account with a selfie video.

entry.title: US on track to have historically low number of homicides in 2026, report says
entry.content: A new report from the Council on Criminal Justice shows an 18% decrease in the homicide rate across 30 American cities from 2025 compared to the same period in 2026

entry.title: Video shows an orca torpedoing toward a sunfish, blowing it into pieces
entry.content: Video shows one orca holding a sunfish by the fin and another torpedoing toward it, blowing it into pieces in the Gulf of California.

entry.title: Google Has the Muscle to Overpower Spending Worries
entry.content: AI investment and delays are concerns, but the company’s core businesses look strong.

entry.title: Bears Are Running Amok in Lake Tahoe and Causing Chaos Among Locals
entry.content: Homeowners spend thousands to ward off the pantry-raiding, toothpaste-sniffing beasts, exposing a rift among volunteer groups.

entry.title: Dubai’s DP World to Build Two New Terminals on U.A.E. East Coast, Bypassing Hormuz
entry.content: The project, agreed in principle with the Fujairah Ports Authority under a 50-year concession, includes a container and multipurpose terminal and a general-cargo terminal.

entry.title: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket is expected to crash into the moon this August
entry.content: The upper stage of a Falcon 9 rocket has been drifting in space for over a year after it launched. It's expected to crash into the moon next month.

entry.title: Two waste paper bins, one Olympic dream
entry.content: Derived from basketball and developed by teenagers in late-Victorian England, netball is sport's great misunderstood "girly game". It could be heading to the Olympics in 2032.

entry.title: Hansi Flick ‘loses trust’ in Barcelona captain after key incident; relationship ‘completely broken’
entry.content: Frenkie de Jong’s future at Barcelona has become a major talking point after fresh reports suggested the midfielder’s relationship with head coach Hansi Flick has deteriorated behind the scenes.Ac...

entry.title: Real Madrid set €60 million spending cap for top midfield target, price tag set at €100 million
entry.content: The past 24 hours have seen the mood around Rodri’s transfer news to Real Madrid fluctuate between optimism and pessimism.There is no doubt that the player is rated highly by the Real Madrid managem...

entry.title: Will Rodri join Real Madrid? Transfer latest on Man City and Spain's World Cup star
entry.content: Spain's World Cup-winning captain is reportedly keen on joining Real Madrid. But could a deal really happen this summer?

entry.title: 2026/27 Football Calendar: Every Key Date for the New Season
entry.content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is now done and dusted, and football fans are fully focused on the upcoming 2026/27 season.Without further ado, read on as we look at all the key dates across the major Europea...

entry.title: Are the Commonwealth Games dying?
entry.content: The Games return this week but spiralling costs, a slimmed-down programme and the weight of its uncomfortable history all put the future of the event, approaching its centenary year, in doubt

entry.title: Dame Sarah Storey: Glasgow 2026 ‘starts a new era for the Commonwealth Games’
entry.content: Interview: Having hung up her wheels, Britain’s most successful Paralympian is now turning to punditry, and believes that despite a difficult build-up Glasgow will put the Commonwealth Games back on the map

entry.title: Is 2026 the Commonwealth Games' last dance or a future-proofing revamp?
entry.content: It's been going for 96 years - but many doubted the 2026 Commonwealth Games, which open in Glasgow on Thursday, would happen. So what could the future hold for the event?

entry.title: WNBA All-Star Weekend schedule 2026: Times, TV channels, lineups for 3-Point Contest, Skills Challenge and more
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know about the 2026 WNBA All-Star Weekend, including TV channel and streaming options for each event.

entry.title: Al Hilal agree deal with West Ham for Crysencio Summerville
entry.content: Al Hilal have agreed a deal with West Ham United to sign winger Crysencio Summerville, according to The Athletic.Both clubs have agreed a deal worth an initial £55 million, with a further £5m in add...

entry.title: Felipe Mora scores in stoppage time to help Timbers escape with 2-2 draw against Dallas
entry.content: Felipe Mora scored two minutes into stoppage time to erase a late go-ahead goal by Dallas' Petar Musa and help the Portland Timbers earn a 2-2 draw on Wednesday night. Mora took a pass from Kristoffer Velde before scoring his second goal of the season for the equalizer for Portland (5-8-3). Velde subbed into the match in the 55th minute before posting his career-high third assist.

entry.title: What Can Sunderland Hope To Gain From Their USA Tour?
entry.content: As the Lads head across the Atlantic, we asked our writers for their thoughts on what they’d like to see!

entry.title: Bay Area sports calendar, July 23-24
entry.content: Defending champion Padraig Harrington is among 156 golfers entered in the 2026 U.S. Senior Open. Coverage begins at 5:30 a.m. Thursday on the Golf Channel.

entry.title: Opposition Focus: Can Sunderland Show Some Pre-Season Promise Against Liverpool?
entry.content: Ahead of Saturday night’s clash in the Music City, Lewis Forbister runs the rule over our opponents!

entry.title: WNBA fans make AI allegations over Caitlin Clark’s image on NBA 2K27 Deluxe Edition
entry.content: NBA 2K’s new Caitlin Clark announcement doesn’t sit well with basketball fans. Indiana Fever superstar Caitlin Clark has once again proven that she’s the face of the WNBA with a recent collaboration with video-gaming giant NBA 2K.

entry.title: Man shot and killed by police in Madison, Wisconsin, sparking protests
entry.content: Madison's chief of police says an officer in Wisconsin's capital city shot and killed a man who had injured him with a knife while resisting arrest.

entry.title: Man embraces remote work by setting up offices at the beach, baseball game
entry.content: A California man is taking advantage of remote work with viral office settings that are attracting fans, critics, and thousands of views. Gwen Baumgarder joined him for a workday at the ballpark.

entry.title: Comforting news for coffee drinkers concerned about their heart health
entry.content: When it comes to heart health.. how many cups of caffeinated coffee are safe to drink each day? A new statement from the American Heart Association may be reassuring to coffee drinkers. Gwen Baumgardner has more.

entry.title: House committee urges DOJ to charge former Trump prosecutor Jack Smith
entry.content: The GOP chair of the House Judiciary Committee encouraged the DOJ to look into criminally charging Jack Smith, alleging the former special counsel who brought charges against President Trump lied to Congress.

entry.title: U.S. carries out more Iran strikes as threats to oil trade deepen
entry.content: President Trump threatened to bomb an Iranian bridge or power plant for every ship attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, as one shipping insider says "nothing is going through."

entry.title: Carmelo Anthony singles out Meleek Thomas as the most impressive rookie at Summer League
entry.content: Meleek Thomas is already proving the teams that passed over him wrong with his Summer League performance.

entry.title: Amish Shah projected to win Arizona's 1st District Democratic primary
entry.content: Democrat Amish Shah will be up against Republican Jay Feely in Arizona's 1st Congressional District in November.

entry.title: Darryn Peterson wanted to send a message during NBA Summer League with the foul limit
entry.content: Darryn Peterson had a lot of fun with a unique NBA Summer League rule.

entry.title: Tropical Storm Bertha makes landfall on Gulf Coast. See its path.
entry.content: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

entry.title: National Guard deployed after West Virginia flash floods dump 7 inches of rain
entry.content: Torrential rains in West Virginia have caused flash flooding that inundated homes and businesses this week.

entry.title: Caitlin Clark adds special Chicago appearance to WNBA All-Star weekend schedule
entry.content: Caitlin Clark declined an invitation to the WNBA 3-Point Contest, but the Fever star has added another major appearance to her All-Star weekend schedule.

entry.title: Sophie Cunningham shares special moment with Brittney Griner before Fever-Sun game
entry.content: Sophie Cunningham and Brittney Griner put the competition aside for a special reunion before the Indiana Fever hosted the Connecticut Sun.

entry.title: Mauricio Pochettino replacements: Ranking the USMNT's candidates, from Pep Guardiola to Pellegrino Matarazzo
entry.content: Now that the 2026 FIFA World Cup has concluded, the future of USMNT head coach Mauricio Pochettino is now up in the air. If the esteemed coach leaves U.S. Soccer, who could replace him?
